mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months following an INDPRO increase, or the signal shows zero predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing downward, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% and UNRATE rises, with accuracy below 50% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader consumer confidence deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >65% of months following a >5% housing starts decline, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained employment expansion and wage growth that supports corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices alongside rising policy rates trigger immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in high-inflation subsamples.
